arts.sum: Newcastle host West Ham on Monday evening in the Premier League. Previews includes team news, predicted lineups and how to watch on TV.

arts.sum: New Manchester United boss watched his side secure a draw against Ipswich Town after taking the lead with less than two minutes on the clock when Marcus Rashford found the net.

arts.sum: Chelsea and Arsenal will have to pay £115m to sign Newcastle United's Alexander Isak, Hamburg consider appointing Ruud van Nistelrooy as their new manager, Jose Mourinho dismisses Fenerbahce links to Cristiano Ronaldo, plus more.

arts.ttl: “Typical” | Callum Wilson will return from injury to face West Ham
arts.sum: Newcastle United and England striker Callum Wilson will return from injury to face West Ham United tomorrow night. The 32-year-old hasn’t played for Newcastle so far in 24/25 after suffering back and hamstring injuries, however, according to Wilson himself, he will be returning to face his favourite opponents this weekend. arts.ttl: Newcastle United transfer warning as striker decision confirmed amid Man Utd and Liverpool link
arts.sum: Newcastle have been reported as having an interest in Lille's Canadian international forward Jonathan David, a player enjoying an impressive season and one who has been tipped for stardom beyond Ligue1. Already the list of players being linked with a move to St James' Park is growing, with Eddie Howe keen to strengthen his squad in January for what is shaping up to be a big push for a Champions League place in what is an already congested Premier League table.

arts.ttl: “Shocking move” | Real Madrid interested in West Ham defender
arts.sum: Real Madrid could approach West Ham United in a move to sign Nayef Aguerd this winter, according to a report from Fichajes. Conference League winner Nayef Aguerd left the Hammers on loan this summer after two seasons and 58 appearances in Claret and Blue. arts.ttl: David Moyes to Everton update as former West Ham boss 'serious contender' amid takeover saga
arts.sum: Former West Ham United manager David Moyes is reportedly being eyed for a return to Premier League football and is on the radar to take over at rivals Everton. It is said the challenge would be an 'appealing' one for Moyes, so long as he receives the correct assurances over a contract, the new owners, their ambitions and the budget he would have to make transfers.
